sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value FLOAT, vals LIST<Integer>);
SELECT argMax(value, id) FROM test_table WHERE vals @> [2, 3] GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(value) > 0;

SELECT a.id, argMax(a.value, a.id) FROM test_table AS a JOIN test_table AS b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.value IS NOT NULL AND b.vals @> [1] GROUP BY a.id HAVING argMax(a.value, a.id) IS NOT NULL ORDER BY argMax(a.value, a.id);
CREATE TABLE another_table (group_id INTEGER, group_value FLOAT, group_vals LIST<Integer>);
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, 5.0, [1]), (1, 7.0, [2, 3]), (3, 4.0, NULL), (3, NULL, [4]);

SELECT argMax(b.group_value, b.group_id) FROM test_table AS a JOIN another_table AS b ON a.id = b.group_id WHERE a.vals @> [3] AND b.group_vals @> [1] GROUP BY b.group_id HAVING argMax(b.group_value, b.group_id) < 6.0;
SELECT argMax(value, id) FROM (SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND vals IS NOT NULL) AS subquery WHERE vals @> [3];
SELECT id, argMax(value, id) FROM (SELECT id, value, vals FROM test_table WHERE NULL IS NULL) AS q WHERE (vals @> [1] OR vals @> [2]) GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(value) > 0;
